text: If one end of diameter of the circle \( x^{2} + y^{2} - 4x - 6y + 11 = 0 \) is \( (3,4) \), then the other end of the diameter is
explanation: Centre of the circle is \( (2,3) \). Let the other end be \( (h,k) \). Then \( \dfrac{3+h}{2} = 2 \) and \( \dfrac{4+k}{2} = 3 \), so \( h=1 \), \( k=2 \).
name: Questions Item 1

text: The equation of a circle with centre \( (5,4) \) and touching the Y-axis is
explanation: Radius equals the x-coordinate of centre (distance to Y-axis) = 5. Equation: \( (x-5)^{2} + (y-4)^{2} = 25 \) simplifies to \( x^{2} + y^{2} - 10x - 8y + 16 = 0 \).
name: Questions Item 2

text: The point on the circle \( x^{2} + y^{2} = 4 \) whose distance from the line \( 4x + 3y - 12 = 0 \) is \( \dfrac{4}{5} \) units is
explanation: Let point be \( (h,k) \) on circle so \( h^{2} + k^{2} = 4 ). Distance condition: \( |4h + 3k - 12| = 4 \). Solving with circle equation yields \( (2,0) \) as valid point.
name: Questions Item 3

text: The equation of the circle passing through \( (0,0) \) and which makes intercepts \( a \) and \( b \) on the coordinate axes is
explanation: General circle through origin with intercepts: centre \( (a/2, b/2) \), radius \( \sqrt{(a/2)^{2} + (b/2)^{2}} \). Equation simplifies to \( x^{2} + y^{2} - ax - by = 0 \).
name: Questions Item 4

text: Find the value of \( m + n \), if the circumference of the circle \( x^{2} + y^{2} - 8x - 8y + m = 0 \) is bisected by the circle \( x^{2} + y^{2} - 2x - 4y + n = 0 \).
explanation: Common chord passes through centre \( (4,4) \) of first circle. Radical axis condition gives \( m + n = -56 \).
name: Questions Item 5

text: The equation of normal at \( (1,1) \) to the circle \( x^{2} + y^{2} - 3x - 4y + 1 = 0 \) is
explanation: Slope of tangent at \( (1,1) \) is \( \dfrac{1-2}{2-3} = 1 \). Normal slope = -1. Equation: \( y - 1 = -1(x - 1) \) simplifies to \( x + y - 2 = 0 \).
name: Questions Item 6

text: The length of the diameter of the circle \( x^{2} + y^{2} - 6x - 8y = 0 \) is .......... units
explanation: Radius = \( \sqrt{9 + 16} = 5 \). Diameter = 10 units.
name: Questions Item 7

text: The length of the tangent drawn from any point on the circle \( x^{2} + y^{2} + gx + fy + c_{1} = 0 \) to the circle \( x^{2} + y^{2} + gx + fy + c_{2} = 0 \) is
explanation: For concentric circles, tangent length = \( \sqrt{r_{1}^{2} - r_{2}^{2}} = \sqrt{c_{2} - c_{1}} \) (adjusted for signs).
name: Questions Item 8

text: If \( 3x + 4y + k = 0 \) is a tangent to the circle \( x^{2} + y^{2} = 10 \), then \( k = \)
explanation: Distance from centre (0,0) to line equals radius: \( |k|/\sqrt{9+16} = \sqrt{10} \) gives \( k = \pm 7 \).
name: Questions Item 9

text: The equations of tangents to the circle \( x^{2} + y^{2} = 10 \) from the point \( (4,-2) \) are
explanation: Pair of tangents from point or solving gives \( 3x + 10y = 0 \), \( x - 3y = 10 \).
name: Questions Item 10

text: If the two circles \( (x-1)^{2} + (y-3)^{2} = r^{2} \) and \( x^{2} + y^{2} - 8x + 2y + 8 = 0 \) intersect in two different points, then
explanation: Distance between centres = 5. Intersection condition: \( |r-3| < 5 < r+3 \) simplifies to \( 2 < r < 8 \).
name: Questions Item 11

text: The equation of the smallest circle passing through the intersection of the line \( x + y = 1 \) and the circle \( x^{2} + y^{2} = 9 \) is
explanation: Family \( x^{2} + y^{2} - 9 + \lambda (x+y-1) = 0 \). Diameter condition \( \lambda = -1 \) gives smallest circle \( x^{2} + y^{2} - (x+y-1) = 0 \).
name: Questions Item 12

text: A circle is drawn touching the X-axis, with its centre at the point of reflection of \( (m,n) \) on the line \( y - x = 0 \). Then the equation of the circle is
explanation: Reflection of \( (m,n) \) is \( (n,m) \). Radius = n (distance to X-axis). Equation: \( (x-n)^{2} + (y-m)^{2} = n^{2} \).
name: Questions Item 13

text: The radical centre of the circles \( x^{2} + y^{2} - 4x - 6y + 11 = 0 \), \( x^{2} + y^{2} - 2x - 4y + 1 = 0 \) and \( x^{2} + y^{2} - 6x - 2y = 0 \) is
explanation: Radical axes intersection gives \( \left( \dfrac{7}{6}, \dfrac{11}{6} \right) \) (no matching option).
name: Questions Item 14

text: Let PQ and RS be tangents at the extremities of a diameter PR of a circle of radius \( r \) such that PS and RQ intersect at a point X on the circumference of the circle, then \( 2r \) equals
explanation: Geometry of tangents and right angles gives \( 2r = PQ \cdot RS \).
name: Questions Item 15

text: The area of an equilateral triangle inscribed in the circle \( x^{2} + y^{2} - 6x - 8y + 12 = 0 \) is sq. units
explanation: Radius = \( \sqrt{34} \). Area = \( \dfrac{3\sqrt{3}}{4} \times (2r \sin 60^{\circ})^{2} = \dfrac{57\sqrt{3}}{2} \).
name: Questions Item 16

text: The equation of the circle with centre \( (2,-3) \) and touching the line \( 3x - 4y + 1 = 0 \) is
explanation: Radius = distance from centre to line = 1. Equation: \( (x-2)^{2} + (y+3)^{2} = 1 \) simplifies to \( x^{2} + y^{2} - 4x - 6y + 12 = 0 \).
name: Questions Item 17

text: Consider the family of circles \( x^{2} + y^{2} - 2x - 4y + \lambda = 0 \) which passes through two fixed points A and B. Distance between them is
explanation: Fixed points solve \( \lambda = 8 \). Distance between intersection points = 6.
name: Questions Item 18

text: The length of the chord intercepted by the circle \( x^{2} + y^{2} - 6x - 8y = 0 \) on the line \( 2x - 5 = 0 \) is equal to ......... units
explanation: Radius = 5, perpendicular distance d=5. Chord length = \( 2\sqrt{r^{2} - d^{2}} = 10 \).
name: Questions Item 19

text: If \( 3x + 4y + k = 0 \) is a tangent to the circle \( x^{2} + y^{2} = 10 \), then \( k = \)
explanation: Distance from origin to line equals radius: \( |k|/\sqrt{25} = \sqrt{10} \) gives \( k = \pm 7 \).
name: Questions Item 20

text: The point on the circle \( x^{2} + y^{2} = 4 \) whose distance from the line \( 4x + 3y - 12 = 0 \) is \( \dfrac{4}{5} \) units is
explanation: Distance condition with circle equation solves to \( (2,0) \).
name: Questions Item 21

text: The equation of the circle passing through \( (0,0) \) and making intercepts \( a \) and \( b \) on axes is
explanation: Centre \( (a/2,b/2) \), radius formula leads to \( x^{2} + y^{2} - ax - by = 0 \).
name: Questions Item 22

text: If \( y = x + \sqrt{k_{1}} \) and \( y = x + \sqrt{k_{2}} \) are two parallel tangents of a circle of radius 2 units, then \( |k_{1} - k_{2}| \) is
explanation: Distance between parallel tangents = diameter = 4. Slope condition gives \( |k_1 - k_2| = 8 \).
name: Questions Item 23

text: The equation of normal at \( (1,1) \) to \( x^{2} + y^{2} - 3x - 4y + 1 = 0 \) is
explanation: Tangent slope = 1, normal slope = -1. Equation passes through point to give \( x + y - 2 = 0 \).
name: Questions Item 24

explanation: Radius = 5, diameter = 10.

explanation: Concentric circles tangent length = \( \sqrt{c_2 - c_1} \).
name: Questions Item 26

text: The equation of normal at \( (1,1) \) to \( x^{2} + y^{2} - 3x - 4y + 1 = 0 \) is
explanation: Tangent slope calculation gives normal \( x + y - 2 = 0 \).

explanation: Radical axes intersect at \( (7/6, 11/6) \) (no option match).

explanation: Intersection points distance calculation gives 6.
